sn6.14:1.2: On one occasion the Blessed One was dwelling in Sāvatthī.
sn6.14:1.5: “Yes, lord,” the monks responded to the Blessed One.
sn6.14:2.2: was a king named Aruṇavant whose royal city was named Aruṇavatī.
sn6.14:2.3: And in dependence on that royal city of Aruṇavatī there dwelled Sikhī the Blessed One, worthy & fully self-awakened.
sn6.14:2.4: And Sikhī the Blessed One, worthy & fully self-awakened, had a pair of disciples named Abhibhū & Sambhava as his foremost pair, his excellent pair.
sn6.14:2.5: “Then Sikhī the Blessed One, worthy & fully self-awakened, addressed the monk Abhibhū:
sn6.14:2.6: ‘Come, brahman, let’s go to a certain Brahmā world until the time for the meal.’
sn6.14:2.7: “‘As you say, lord,’ the monk Abhibhū responded to Sikhī the Blessed One, worthy & fully self-awakened.
sn6.14:2.8: “Then—just as a strong man might extend his flexed arm or flex his extended arm—Sikhī the Blessed One, worthy & fully self-awakened, & the monk Abhibhū disappeared from the royal city of Aruṇavatī and reappeared in that Brahmā world.
sn6.14:3.1: “Then Sikhī the Blessed One, worthy & fully self-awakened, addressed the monk Abhibhū:
sn6.14:3.2: ‘Let a Dhamma talk come to you, brahman, for Brahmā, Brahmā’s retinue, & Brahmā’s assembly.’
sn6.14:3.3: “Responding, ‘As you say, lord,’ to Sikhī the Blessed One, worthy & fully self-awakened, the monk Abhibhū instructed, urged, roused, & encouraged Brahmā, Brahmā’s retinue, & Brahmā’s assembly with a Dhamma talk.
sn6.14:3.4: “With that, Brahmā, Brahmā’s retinue, & Brahmā’s assembly were indignant, annoyed, & complained:
sn6.14:3.5: ‘Isn’t it amazing, good sirs! Isn’t it astounding! How can this disciple, in the presence of his teacher, teach the Dhamma?’
sn6.14:4.1: “Then Sikhī the Blessed One, worthy & fully self-awakened, addressed the monk Abhibhū:
sn6.14:4.2: ‘Brahmā, Brahmā’s retinue, & Brahmā’s assembly are indignant & annoyed with you.
sn6.14:4.3: They complain: “Isn’t it amazing, good sirs! Isn’t it astounding! How can this disciple, in the presence of his teacher, teach the Dhamma?”
sn6.14:4.4: Very well then, strike terror in Brahmā, Brahmā’s retinue, & Brahmā’s assembly to an even greater degree.’
sn6.14:4.5: “Responding, ‘As you say, lord,’ to Sikhī the Blessed One, worthy & fully self-awakened, the monk Abhibhū taught the Dhamma with his body visible, taught the Dhamma with his body invisible, taught the Dhamma with the lower half of his body visible & the upper half invisible, taught the Dhamma with the upper half of his body visible & the lower half invisible.
sn6.14:4.6: “With that, the minds of Brahmā, Brahmā’s retinue, & Brahmā’s assembly were overcome with amazement & astonishment:
sn6.14:4.7: ‘Isn’t it amazing, good sirs! Isn’t it astounding!—the contemplative’s great power & great might!’
sn6.14:5.1: “Then the monk Abhibhū said to Sikhī the Blessed One, worthy & fully self-awakened,
sn6.14:5.2: ‘I recall, lord, having made this statement while standing in the midst of the Saṅgha of monks:
sn6.14:5.3: “I can, friends, while standing in the Brahmā world, make my voice heard throughout the thousand-fold cosmos.”’
sn6.14:5.4: “‘This is the time, brahman! This is the time, brahman,
sn6.14:5.5: for you, while standing in the Brahmā world, to make your voice heard throughout the thousand-fold cosmos!’
sn6.14:5.6: “Responding, ‘As you say, lord,’ to Sikhī the Blessed One, worthy & fully self-awakened, the monk Abhibhū, while standing in the Brahmā world, recited these verses:
sn6.14:6.2: Commit yourselves to the Awakened One’s bidding.
sn6.14:6.4: as an elephant would a shed made of reeds.
sn6.14:7.1: He who, in this Dhamma & Vinaya,
sn6.14:7.4: will put an end to suffering & stress.’
sn6.14:8.1: “Then—having struck terror in Brahmā, Brahmā’s retinue, & Brahmā’s assembly—Sikhī the Blessed One, worthy & fully self-awakened, & the monk Abhibhū—
sn6.14:8.2: just as a strong man might extend his flexed arm or flex his extended arm—disappeared from that Brahmā world and reappeared in the royal city of Aruṇavatī.
sn6.14:8.3: “Then, monks, Sikhī the Blessed One, worthy & fully self-awakened, addressed the monks,
sn6.14:8.4: ‘Monks, did you hear the monk Abhibhū reciting verses while standing in the Brahmā world?’
sn6.14:8.5: “‘Lord, we heard the monk Abhibhū reciting verses while standing in the Brahmā world.’
sn6.14:8.6: “‘But what verses did you hear the monk Abhibhū reciting while standing in the Brahmā world?’
sn6.14:8.7: “‘Lord, we heard the monk Abhibhū reciting these verses while standing in the Brahmā world:
sn6.14:9.2: Commit yourselves to the Awakened One’s bidding.
sn6.14:9.4: as an elephant would a shed made of reeds.
sn6.14:10.1: He who, in this Dhamma & Vinaya,
sn6.14:10.4: will put an end to suffering & stress.’
sn6.14:11.1: “‘These, lord, are the verses we heard the monk Abhibhū reciting while standing in the Brahmā world.’
sn6.14:11.3: It’s good that you heard the monk Abhibhū reciting verses while standing in the Brahmā world.’”
sn6.14:12.1: That is what the Blessed One said. Gratified, the monks delighted in the Blessed One’s words.
